---
title: "LRPITT27: Site-adjusted modified-ITT offer effect on word reading (W)"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

{{< include _partials/_header.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_setup.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_gate_badge.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_key_findings.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_reading_guide.qmd >}}

## Overview

LRPITT27 is the **site-adjusted available-case modified-ITT** analysis for word reading (EWRSWR) (`ewrswr`)—the site-robustness companion to LRPITT10. It adds study site (`area`: 1 = North, 2 = South; 30 / 24 children in the repository). Unlike the SES covariates, `area` is fully observed, so it causes no additional exclusion beyond the word-reading analytic requirement; LRPITT27 and LRPITT10 use the same outcome-available rows. The estimand is the randomised offer effect on $W_{\text{post}}$ in that fitted sample, conditional on ignorable exclusion and missingness—not a full-population effect for all 57 randomised children.

_Identification and sign convention._ Under the locked causal DAG, $G$ is a randomised root, so randomisation identifies the offer contrast $\tau$ within the outcome-available fitted sample without adjustment. Generalising beyond that sample requires ignorable exclusion and missingness. The own baseline, linear age, and the
site term are **precision / robustness terms only** — they sharpen $\tau$ and probe
its stability, but are not what licenses the causal interpretation.
**Sign convention:** $G = 1$ is the intervention arm, so a **positive** $\tau$ means
the intervention raises the outcome.

## Model

$$
\eta_i = \alpha + \tau\, G_i
       + \gamma_{\text{own}}\, \text{logit}_*(W_{\mathrm{pre},i}, N)
       + \gamma_A\, A_{\text{std},i} + \gamma_{\text{area}}\, z(\text{area}_i),
$$

with Beta-Binomial likelihood $W_{\text{post},i} \sim \text{BetaBinomial}(N, \mu_i\kappa, (1-\mu_i)\kappa)$,
$\mu_i = \sigma(\eta_i)$, $N = 79$. The site term enters as a linear $\gamma_{\text{area}}$
(the same weak $\text{Normal}(0, 0.3)$ prior as cross-baseline couplings); HSGPs are
off by default. Priors follow the shared template in `priors.py`.

![Model diagram](model_graph.png){#fig-model .lightbox fig-align="left"}

{{< include _partials/_priors.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_prior_predictive.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_results_itt.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_technical.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_footer.qmd >}}
